report.summary: Built 2 new learn guides: Smart Contract Security Audit Guide 2026 and Celestia vs EigenDA vs Avail DA Layer Comparison 2026, both added to PUBLISHED_PAGES

sections.title: Research Brief
items: Smart contract security: $17B lost to crypto hacks in 2025, $3.9B+ from smart contract exploits. OWASP Smart Contract Top 10 2026 identifies access control ($953M losses), input validation (34.6% of exploits), reentrancy, oracle manipulation as top vulnerabilities. Major incidents: Bybit $1.5B (operational), Cetus $223M (integer overflow), GMX $42M (reentrancy). 70% of incidents catchable by proper audits.

Data availability race: Celestia holds ~50% DA market share (160GB+ processed), EigenDA V2 achieves 100MB/s throughput backed by $335M+ restaked assets from EigenLayer's $19B TVL, Avail combines DAS + KZG commitments for multichain flexibility. Celestia Matcha upgrade doubles blocks to 128MB, Fibre protocol targets 1Tbps. EigenDA uses DAC model (trust tradeoff for throughput), Mantle+Celo ($3.06B TVL) are key adopters.

Gap analysis: No existing smart contract audit/security content despite it being a critical trust topic. Individual DA guides exist (Celestia, DA layers) but no head-to-head comparison of the three leading solutions.

sections.title: Content Created
items: Smart Contract Security Audits: Complete Guide 2026 — 789 lines, ~3200 words. Covers OWASP Top 10 vulnerability table, audit process breakdown, audit firm comparison (OpenZeppelin, Trail of Bits, Sherlock, CertiK, Cyfrin), cost analysis ($5K-$500K), real exploit case studies, DYOR guide for regular users, 6 FAQs.

Celestia vs EigenDA vs Avail: DA Layer Comparison 2026 — 511 lines, ~3000 words. Deep dives on each DA solution, architecture/security model analysis, head-to-head comparison table (7 dimensions), cost economics, selection framework by use case, future outlook, 6 FAQs.

sections.title: SEO Optimization
items: Page 1 — Title: 'Smart Contract Security Audits: Complete Guide 2026' (51 chars). Keywords: 'smart contract audit', 'smart contract security', 'DeFi security', 'OWASP smart contract'. Schemas: Article + FAQ (6 items). OG + Twitter cards configured.

Page 2 — Title: 'Celestia vs EigenDA vs Avail: DA Layer Comparison 2026' (55 chars). Keywords: 'data availability', 'Celestia vs EigenDA', 'Avail DA', 'DA layer comparison'. Schemas: Article + FAQ (6 items). OG + Twitter cards configured.

Internal linking: Page 1 links to common-crypto-scams, flash-loans-guide, defi-liquidation-protection, mev-protection-guide, zero-knowledge-proofs-guide. Page 2 links to data-availability-layer-guide, celestia-guide, ethereum-blob-space-economics, cross-chain-interoperability-guide, zero-knowledge-proofs-guide.

notes.publish_queue_note: Both pages added directly to PUBLISHED_PAGES (batch 6 for 2026-03-28) since PUBLISH_QUEUE only contains 2 blocked items needing slug fixes.
notes.build_fix: Removed 'use client' directive from both pages (incompatible with metadata export). Fixed JSX escape for '>' character in DA comparison page. Fixed 'marginmarginBottom' typo.
